audit: .work/insights/WORK-0023-release-version-validation-audit.md — next_version called outside main()'s try/except; non-canonical --current-version -> unhandled IndexError/ValueError, no JSON. REAL -> FIX.

cycle_1.historian: PASS — audit pattern followed; plain ValueError appropriate for this simpler surface; envelope shape consistent.
cycle_1.edge-case-hunter: CONDITIONAL — BLOCKING: `^...$` allowed a trailing newline ("1.2.3\n" passed). SIGNIFICANT: leading zeros coerced silently. MINOR: unknown-bump fallthrough; TypeError on {"completed": null}.
cycle_1.simplicity-enforcer: CONDITIONAL — claimed re.ASCII unnecessary (incorrect: \d matches Unicode digits without it; kept). Redundant validation in main+next_version (kept as defense-in-depth). Verbose message (kept).

fixes: Regex now `^(0|[1-9]\d*)\.(...)\.(...)\Z` with re.ASCII: `\Z` rejects trailing newline/whitespace (BLOCKING); no-leading-zero identifiers reject 01.2.3 (SIGNIFICANT).
next_version raises ValueError on an unknown bump (was a silent patch fallthrough).
main()'s work-json except now includes TypeError so {"completed": null} yields a JSON envelope, not a traceback.
Tests extended: "1.2.3\n", leading zeros, unknown bump.

cycle_2.edge-case-hunter: PASS — all 3 prior findings RESOLVED; \Z rejects \n/\r\n/trailing-space; (0|[1-9]\d*) rejects leading zeros, accepts 0.0.0. New findings MINOR only.

accepted_minor: _NUM capturing-group positional indices (group 1/2/3) — works; fragility hypothetical, documented by the _NUM comment.
Empty completed list -> patch (mis-typed --phase) and group_by_class dropping an unknown change_class from output: pre-existing, about --phase / change_class, not --current-version (WORK-0023 scope).
